Process EM-DAT Flood Events Into a Country-Week Panel

Source: R/process_EMDAT_data.R
process_EMDAT_data.Rd

Reads the most recent EM-DAT public-extract xlsx file in PATHS$DATA_EMDAT_RAW, filters to flood events in MOSAIC AFRO countries, expands each event across the ISO-8601 weeks it spans, and writes a complete country-week panel of flood-occurrence covariates to PATHS$DATA_EMDAT/floods_country_weekly.csv.

Usage

process_EMDAT_data(PATHS)

Arguments

PATHS

A list of paths generated by get_paths(). Must include:

  • DATA_EMDAT_RAW: Path to MOSAIC-data/raw/EMDAT.

  • DATA_EMDAT: Path to the processed output directory (MOSAIC-data/processed/EMDAT/weekly).

Value

Invisibly returns the path to the written CSV. The CSV contains one row per (iso_code, year, week) for every ISO-week between date_start (default 2000-01-03) and the latest week covered by any flood event in the raw extract, restricted to iso_codes_mosaic. Columns:

iso_code

ISO3 country code.

year

ISO-8601 year of the week.

week

ISO-8601 week number (1-53).

date_start

Monday of the ISO-week.

date_stop

Sunday of the ISO-week.

emdat_flood_active

1 if any flood event overlaps the week, else 0.

emdat_flood_new

Number of flood events that start in this week.

emdat_flood_affected

log1p of summed Total Affected across overlapping events (NAs treated as 0).

emdat_flood_deaths

log1p of summed Total Deaths across overlapping events (NAs treated as 0).

Details

EM-DAT records carry frequent missing day-of-month and occasional missing end-month values. Imputation rules:

  • Missing Start Day or End Day -> 15.

  • Missing End Month -> Start Month.

  • Missing End Year -> Start Year.

  • If the resulting end_date is earlier than start_date (data-entry artifact), it is reset to start_date.

Each event is expanded to the unique set of ISO-8601 (year, week) pairs touched by the daily date range start_date:end_date. The panel grid is the full Cartesian product of iso_codes_mosaic and all ISO-weeks from date_start through the latest event end-week; cells with no flood activity carry zeros, so a downstream left-join against the suitability table does not need to impute missingness.

EM-DAT only records events meeting CRED's inclusion thresholds (>=10 deaths, >=100 affected, or formal emergency declaration), so this covariate captures major flood occurrences rather than continuous flood intensity. The richer continuous signal lives in the OpenMeteo precipitation/anomaly covariates.

On this page
